skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X workflows through the inference.sh CLI, including posting, media posts, engagement actions, direct messages, follows, and profile lookups.
skill.summary: Run Twitter/X posting and engagement workflows with documented inference.sh CLI commands.
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: The static command findings are mostly documented examples for the allowed infsh CLI, not arbitrary shell execution or command injection. The skill still enables real Twitter/X actions and external service calls, so it should publish with warnings about account authorization, network activity, and irreversible social actions.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Can Perform Account Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access to infsh and documents commands that can post tweets, create media posts, like, retweet, delete posts, send DMs, and follow users. This is a legitimate automation purpose, but misuse or mistaken prompts could create public posts or private messages from a connected account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and examples directly show infsh commands for Twitter/X account actions. The risk is contextual misuse rather than command injection, because the documented commands are fixed CLI invocations.

medium_findings.title: Network-Backed Third-Party Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill depends on inference.sh and links to hosted media and documentation URLs. These are expected for the integration, but users should know the workflow sends content and account actions through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly references inference.sh and documents app runs that require external service access. I did not find evidence of hidden exfiltration or unrelated network destinations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts point to descriptive Markdown content and table headings, not code that imports or calls a cryptographic algorithm. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak cryptography use was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows prose metadata and a Markdown table header. There is no executable crypto code at those locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Example Inputs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL matches are normal references to inference.sh documentation, a hosted image, and a placeholder media URL. They do not show secret leakage or suspicious data transfer by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or sample values tied to the stated Twitter/X integration. No evidence found that they receive credentials or local data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed infsh Shell Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows Bash(infsh *) and provides many infsh app run examples. This is the intended mechanism, but it allows state-changing external account operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command permission and examples are explicit in the skill file. The pattern is risky because it performs external actions, not because of hidden shell injection.

content.value_statement: Managing X posts and engagement by hand takes time. This skill provides ready commands for posting, media updates, DMs, follows, and profile lookups.
actual_capabilities: Posts text tweets through the x/post-tweet app.
Creates Twitter/X posts with media through x/post-create.
Likes, retweets, deletes, and retrieves posts by ID.
Sends direct messages and follows users with documented app commands.
Retrieves user profile data by username.
Shows workflows that combine AI-generated images or videos with posting.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Does not include rate limit handling or queue management logic.
Cannot guarantee that posts comply with platform rules.
Examples rely on external inference.sh apps and network access.

use_cases.title: Publish Product Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare and post short launch updates or media posts from a connected Twitter/X account.

use_cases.title: Manage Engagement Tasks
use_cases.description: Like, retweet, follow, or retrieve posts during a controlled social media workflow.

use_cases.title: Prototype Social Integrations
use_cases.description: Test Twitter/X posting and profile lookup flows before building a custom integration.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Post this approved update to our Twitter/X account: [message]. Confirm the text before running the command.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Media Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Twitter/X post using this approved caption and media URL. Check the URL and caption length before posting.

prompt_templates.title: Review Engagement Targets
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these tweet IDs and propose which ones to like or retweet. Wait for approval before taking action.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Generated Media Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a campaign asset, extract the hosted media URL, draft the post text, and ask for approval before publishing.

output_examples.input: Post an approved product update to Twitter/X.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the final text, runs the posting workflow, and reports the resulting post status or identifier.

output_examples.input: Create a media post from a caption and hosted image URL.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies the caption and media link, creates the post, and summarizes whether publication succeeded.

output_examples.input: Look up a user profile before following them.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves the profile information, summarizes the account, and asks for approval before following.

best_practices: Confirm every public post, DM, follow, like, retweet, and deletion before execution.
Use approved account credentials and document who authorized each workflow.
Review platform rules and rate limits before running repeated engagement actions.
anti_patterns: Do not run unattended engagement bots from broad or vague prompts.
Do not post secrets, private customer data, or unreleased company information.
Do not use the delete command without verifying the exact post ID.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to Twitter/X?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses inference.sh apps that can create posts from an authenticated account.

faq.question: Does it require another tool?
faq.answer: Yes. The inference.sh CLI must be installed and logged in before commands will work.

faq.question: Can it post images or videos?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents media posting through x/post-create with a hosted media URL.

faq.question: Can it manage engagement actions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for likes, retweets, follows, direct messages, and post deletion.

faq.question: Is it safe for unattended automation?
faq.answer: No. Public posts and account actions should require review and explicit approval.

faq.question: Does the audit find malicious code?
faq.answer: No evidence of malicious intent was found. The main risk is authorized external account automation.
